content.claim: Cohort decline is structurally embedded for at least 20+ years due to dual mechanism: declining total fertility rate per woman combined with shrinking pool of reproductive-age women, independent of short-term behavioral fluctuations.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Long-term population projections contain inherent uncertainty beyond 10-15 years
Policy interventions (e.g., family support, migration policy) could alter maternal cohort size trajectory
Assumes no major exogenous demographic shocks
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
